title: IV. The genealogy and successors of Sivāchārya. Suppression of the great sacerdotal families by Sūryavarman I - Persée
highlights:
THE GENEALOGY AND SUCCESSORS OF SIVÀCHÀRYA SUPPRESSION OF THE GREAT SACERDOTAL FAMILIES BY SÛRYAVARMAN I
by
Lawrence Palmer BRIGGS The early kings of Cambodia were served by members of great sacerdotal families which had been granted hereditary —sometimes exclusive —rights to furnish certain functionaries. Thus, the family of Sivakaivalya had the exclusive right of furnishing purohitas of the Devarâja. The family of Pranavâtman had the hereditary right to furnish royal hotars.  ...    ### The Identity of the Sivácháryas of the Inscriptions of Sdok КакThom, Vat Thïpdëi Вand Ta Kèv В. Four inscriptions mention one or more functionaries named Sivâchârya who in one or another of these charges served various kings from léânavarman II (99Д- 928) to Suryavarman I (1 ooa-i o5o).
